subtopics: Need for measurement; Units of measurement; Systems of units
key_concepts: SI units, fundamental and derived units
Significant figures
Determining the uncertainty in result
Dimensions of physical quantities
Dimensional analysis and its applications

subtopics: Frame of reference; Motion in a straight line
key_concepts: Elementary concepts of differentiation and integration for describing motion
Uniform and non-uniform motion
Average speed, average velocity, instantaneous velocity
Uniformly accelerated motion
Velocity-time and position-time graphs; Relations for uniformly accelerated motion (graphical and calculus treatment)

subtopics: Scalar and vector quantities; Position and displacement vectors
key_concepts: General vectors, equality of vectors, multiplication by real number
Addition and subtraction of vectors; Unit vector; Resolution in a plane
Rectangular components; Scalar and Vector product
Motion in a plane: uniform velocity and acceleration
Projectile motion; Uniform circular motion

subtopics: Intuitive concept of force; Inertia
key_concepts: Newton's first law (Law of inertia)
Momentum and Newton's second law; Impulse
Newton's third law; Law of conservation of linear momentum
Equilibrium of concurrent forces
Static, kinetic, rolling friction; Lubrication

subtopics: Work done by constant and variable force
key_concepts: Kinetic energy; Work-energy theorem
Power
Potential energy (including spring); Conservative vs non-conservative forces
Motion in a vertical circle
Elastic and inelastic collisions (1D and 2D)

subtopics: Kepler's laws of planetary motion
key_concepts: Universal law of gravitation
Acceleration due to gravity and its variation with altitude/depth
Gravitational potential energy and gravitational potential
Escape speed; Orbital velocity of a satellite
Energy of an orbiting satellite

subtopics: Elasticity; Stress-strain relationship
key_concepts: Hooke's law
Young’s modulus, bulk modulus, shear modulus (qualitative), Poisson's ratio
Elastic energy
Application of elastic behavior (qualitative)

subtopics: Pressure due to fluid column; Pascal's law
key_concepts: Viscosity, Stokes' law, terminal velocity
Streamline and turbulent flow; Critical velocity
Bernoulli's theorem (Torricelli's law, Dynamic lift)
Surface energy and surface tension; Angle of contact
Excess pressure across curved surface; Drops, bubbles, capillary rise

subtopics: Heat, temperature; Thermal expansion (solids, liquids, gases)
key_concepts: Anomalous expansion of water
Specific heat capacity (Cp, Cv); Calorimetry
Change of state - latent heat
Heat transfer: conduction, convection, radiation; Thermal conductivity
Blackbody radiation concepts: Wein's displacement Law, Stefan's law

subtopics: Thermal equilibrium; Zeroth law of thermodynamics
key_concepts: Heat, work and internal energy
First law of thermodynamics

Change of gaseous state conditions (isothermal, adiabatic)
Reversible, irreversible, and cyclic processes

key_concepts: Kinetic theory assumptions and concept of pressure

Degrees of freedom; Law of equipartition of energy (statement)
Application to specific heat capacities of gases
Mean free path; Avogadro's number

subtopics: Periodic motion: time period, frequency
key_concepts: Displacement as a function of time; Periodic functions and applications
Simple harmonic motion (S.H.M)
Uniform circular motion and its equations
Content not explicitly detailed in provided text excerpt

subtopics: Conservation of charge, Force between point charges, Continuous charge distribution

key_concepts: Electric dipole and torque in uniform field
Gauss's law for symmetric distributions (wire, plane, shell)

subtopics: Potential due to point charge/dipole, Equipotential surfaces, Potential energy of systems
Conductors/Insulators, Capacitance combinations (series/parallel), Parallel plate capacitor with dielectric
key_concepts: Energy stored in a capacitor (formulae only)
Dielectrics and electric polarization

topics: Drift of Charges and Ohm's Law
subtopics: Current, Drift velocity, Mobility, Resistivity/Conductivity, Temperature dependence

key_concepts: V-I characteristics (linear and non-linear)
Kirchhoff's rules applications
